response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile engineer with 15+ years of software development, but their profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. Professional C++ experience is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project, whereas the role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise. Their systems-level strengths (low-latency DLang services, event-driven platforms) are adjacent but not substitutable, and there is no evidence of IEC 62443/SDL practice, MQTT/industrial protocols, or automation-industry exposure.
strengths: Broad, senior-level engineering experience across web, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Proven low-latency, concurrent systems work (async I/O, fibers, micro-services at millions of requests/sec)
Strong craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience, and none recent — the core requirement of the role
No industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443), SDL, MQTT, or automation-protocol background
Career trajectory centers on web/full-stack and DLang, suggesting a poor domain fit and interest mismatch

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent professional C++ expertise
No industrial automation or IEC 62443 security background
